opinion.h4: ANDERSON ET AL. v. BALL, COUNTY TREASURER. APPEAL FROM THE SUPREME COURT OF ILLINOIS. No. 326. Decided October 23, 1961.
opinion.p: The motion to dismiss is granted and the appeal is dismissed. Treating the papers whereon the appeal was taken as a petition for writ of certiorari, certiorari is denied.
opinion.h4: TINSLEY v. CITY OF RICHMOND, 368 U.S. 18 (1961)
opinion.p: 368 U.S. 18 TINSLEY v. CITY OF RICHMOND. APPEAL FROM THE SUPREME COURT OF APPEALS OF VIRGINIA. No. 315. Decided October 23, 1961.
Appeal dismissed for want of a substantial federal question.
Martin A. Martin, Thurgood Marshall, Jack Greenberg, James M. Nabrit III and Charles L. Black, Jr. for appellant.
The motion to dismiss is granted and the appeal is dismissed for want of a substantial federal question.
MR. JUSTICE DOUGLAS is of the opinion that probable jurisdiction should be noted.
